intro.title: Ninety Days to Carthage
intro.body: Justinian sent Belisarius with 15,000 men and a fleet of 500 ships to reclaim Africa from the Vandals. Procopius, who sailed with him, gives the day-by-day account in Wars III-IV. The campaign was won in two battles inside three months.

stops.name: Constantinople
stops.note: Embarkation, 21 June 533. The patriarch Epiphanius blesses the lead ship from the harbour mole.
stops.imagePrompt: Hand-coloured plate. Golden Horn crowded with masts at midsummer, patriarch in vestments raising the cross over the fleet.

stops.name: Methone
stops.note: Peloponnesian provisioning stop. Procopius records bad biscuit killing 500 men here — bakers had reused old grain.
stops.imagePrompt: Hand-coloured plate. Small harbour below a Venetian-style fortress (anachronistic — original was simpler), ships taking on water.

stops.name: Caput Vada
stops.note: Landfall on the African coast, late August 533. Belisarius disembarks fully equipped and marches inland.
stops.imagePrompt: Hand-coloured plate. Long sandy headland, troops marching from beached transports, dust column inland.

stops.name: Ad Decimum
stops.note: 13 September 533, ten miles south of Carthage. Belisarius wins the first Vandal battle by intercepting Gelimer's encirclement.
stops.imagePrompt: Hand-coloured plate. Roman milestone with the numeral 'X' (anachronistic — original was Roman numeric stone), running battle in olive groves.

stops.name: Carthage
stops.note: Belisarius enters 15 September 533. He dines that night in Gelimer's own palace, off the Vandal king's own plate (Procopius III.21).
stops.imagePrompt: Hand-coloured plate. Carthage harbour rings, byzantine general dining beneath frescoes, Vandal banner being lowered outside.
